results.name: LT Trust distribution — wire/overnight delivery options
source_articles: LT: How to Request a 401(k) Termination Cash Withdrawal or Rollover

request_payload.inquiry: I need my 401(k) money as fast as possible. What are my fastest delivery options and what do they cost?

response_key_points: A $75 distribution request fee applies to all distribution requests.
Wire transfer is typically completed within 1–2 weeks and has an additional $35 wire fee.
OverNight Check delivery is typically completed within 1–2 weeks and has an additional $35 fee, but it requires a physical street address because UPS cannot deliver overnight checks to P.O. boxes.
A standard check usually arrives within 2–3 weeks via USPS and does not have an additional delivery charge beyond the distribution request fee.
If you choose wire delivery, you must use wire-specific instructions from the receiving institution because many financial institutions use different routing details for wires versus ACH.
If a wire is returned or an ACH is rejected, the payment may be converted to a mailed check sent to your home address on file.

For a cash distribution, 20% federal mandatory withholding applies, state withholding may also apply depending on state law, and the cash distribution is taxable income for the calendar year processed.
results.response_outcome_reason: The inquiry is retirement-plan related, and the participant data shows Terminated status, a termination date of 2026-02-01, no plan blackout period, and a verified Total Vested Balance of $12,000, which is above the $75 threshold for the normal distribution process.
results.response_opening: Based on your Terminated status, termination date of 2026-02-01, and $12,000 Total Vested Balance, you can request a termination distribution through ForUsAll. For fastest delivery, the relevant options are wire transfer or OverNight Check, but your P.O. Box affects whether an overnight check can be used.

response_steps.action: Log in to the ForUsAll participant portal
response_steps.detail: Go to forusall.com/login. For best results, use a computer or laptop with an up-to-date Chrome or Edge browser.
From the dashboard, select Loans & Distributions. If you have more than one ForUsAll 401(k), confirm you are accessing the correct plan.
Choose Separation of Service. If the portal shows Default instead due to the known website issue, select Default and continue because it is the same option.

response_steps.action: Review your account sources and vesting
response_steps.detail: Only vested balances are eligible for distribution. If the vesting information looks incorrect, contact ForUsAll Support before submitting.
Expand Email Confirmation Information and enter at least one valid email address for updates or inquiries.

response_steps.action: Choose the distribution type and delivery method
response_steps.detail: Select the option that matches what you want: lump sum cash distribution, full direct rollover, or partial direct rollover with the remaining balance as cash. For fastest cash access, compare wire transfer against OverNight Check, keeping in mind that OverNight Check cannot be sent to your P.O. Box.

For a cash wire, enter the required routing and account details using wire-specific instructions. For an OverNight Check, provide a physical street address. For a regular cash check, provide a mailing address.
Confirm your distribution election, tax withholding, and delivery method before submitting. ForUsAll reviews requests within about two business days.

response_warnings: The additional $35 fee for wire transfer or OverNight Check is non-refundable.
Incorrect wire instructions may cause funds to be rejected and reissued by check, which can add delays.

response_questions.question: Are you enrolled in MFA and able to log in to the ForUsAll portal?
response_questions.why: Portal submission is the preferred and fastest route; if portal access is not possible because of MFA or login issues, ForUsAll Support can provide a RightSignature electronic form as a slower secondary option.

response_questions.question: If you want to use OverNight Check, what physical street address should be used?
response_questions.why: Overnight checks are sent by UPS and cannot be delivered to a P.O. Box.

response_questions.question: If you want to use wire transfer, have you confirmed the wire-specific routing and account instructions directly with your receiving financial institution?
response_questions.why: Wire transfers require exact instructions to avoid rejection, return, or conversion to a mailed check.

response_guardrails: Did not treat the missing participant name as blocking because the inquiry asks for options, costs, and timelines and the core eligibility data is available.
Did not state that unvested funds are eligible; only the verified Total Vested Balance was used.
Did not promise a faster completion time than the timelines provided in the knowledge base.
Did not say an OverNight Check can be delivered to a P.O. Box.
Did not guarantee that wire delivery will succeed if instructions are incorrect.
response_data_gaps: MFA enrollment and current portal login ability were not provided.
Wire-specific bank instructions were not provided.
A physical street address was not provided for potential OverNight Check delivery.
